items.title: BMO rolls out financial navigation tool
items.desc: The US arm of BMO has launched an app developed by fintech MSN Holding designed to help customers boost their financial health.

items.title: Making stablecoins stable(r): can regulation help?
items.desc: by Tirupam Goel, Ulf Lewrick, Isha AgarwalWe model a stablecoin issuer that optimises capital, cash and bond holdings under persistent stablecoin flows. Absent regulation, the issuer holds little capital and favours interest-bearing but less-liquid bonds over cash.

items.title: Paymentology CTO Says Meta Program Shows Limits of Stablecoin Payouts
items.desc: Meta announced in March it would begin offering stablecoin payouts to Colombian and Philippines-based creators. But Tim Joslyn, chief technology officer at Paymentology, argued in a CoinDesk opinion piece Saturday (June 6) that this was “not a complete payments experience” but “a faster way to move money between accounts.” While stablecoins have “largely solved […] The post Paymentology CTO Says Meta Program Shows Limits of Stablecoin Payouts appeared first on PYMNTS.com.

items.title: Crypto World Braces for Failures as Bitcoin Falls Below $60K
items.desc: Bitcoin dropped below $60,000 last week, its lowest price in two years. And against this backdrop, another downturn is happening across the larger cryptocurrency market, Bloomberg News reported Sunday (June 7), wiping out billions of dollars. The report cited a recent video from Charles Hoskinson, founder of the Cardano crypto network, who warned users […] The post Crypto World Braces for Failures as Bitcoin Falls Below $60K appeared first on PYMNTS.com.

items.title: NY judge stays lawsuit seeking ownership of nearly 40,000 bitcoin wallets, sets July hearing on proposed amicus brief
items.desc: The brief, filed by attorney Ian R. Cohen, argues New York's lost-and-found statute cannot be used to claim "lost" assets controlled by private keys.

items.title: What happens to Bitcoin if the Nasdaq falls further?
items.desc: Bitcoin eyes a rally toward $92,630 as BTC defends key long-term support while the Nasdaq flashes deeper correction risks.

items.title: A quick review of the Ways and Means tax bills: State of Crypto
items.desc: The House Ways and Means Committee is gearing up for its big tax push.

items.title: Strategy’s Saylor signals BTC buy as preferred dividend pay date vote looms
items.desc: Strategy’s top execs took to social media on Sunday to tout its Bitcoin strategy while shareholders cast final votes on a proposal for twice-monthly preferred stock dividend payments.

items.title: Michael Saylor revives bitcoin-buy speculation as scrutiny over Strategy grows
items.desc: The firm's executive chairman posted a familiar chart with Strategy's previous BTC purchases writing "a good time to add more dots."

items.title: JPMorgan says Strategy may need to rebuild dollar reserves to restore confidence
items.desc: JPMorgan said crypto's outlook depends on Strategy's dividend plan and the Clarity Act, for which it now sees less than a 50% chance of passage this year.

items.title: Bitcoin near $60,000 today vs February: Institutional sentiment has flipped
items.desc: Bitcoin’s return to $60,000 is drawing heavy ETF outflows, marking a sharp reversal from February when institutional selling eased into the dip.

items.title: Wall Street Is Coming for Hyperliquid's Perps Crown, Arthur Hayes Says
items.desc: Arthur Hayes warned that Hyperliquid's core value driver—using trading fees to burn tokens—exposes the protocol to market share losses.

items.title: Abra’s Bill Barhydt says Wall Street’s next crypto bet is tokenization
items.desc: As Abra prepares for a Nasdaq debut, CEO Bill Barhydt is betting tokenized yield products and onchain lending will drive the next phase of crypto wealth management.

items.title: Bitcoin risks new 'purge' with bear-market losses still $35B below 2022 total
items.desc: Bitcoin realized losses remained below the $211 billion tally from 2022, leading to a prediction that the next bear-market bottom was not yet in.

items.title: How to Get Crypto Exposure Without Owning Crypto Directly
items.desc: Spot or index ETFs are good ways for ordinary investors to add cryptocurrencies to their portfolios, and there are higher-end options for the wealthy.

items.title: Claude Opus 4.8 Review: Better At What’s It Good At, Worse At What It’s Not
items.desc: Anthropic's new flagship aced our math problem and shipped a spotless game—then drained our entire token quota in a single prompt. We ran it through six tests, and here's how it did.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation cuts and departures aren't a crisis, Joe Lubin says
items.desc: The Consensys leader told CoinDesk the Ethereum Foundation's role should be narrower and more focused on stewarding the network's core technology and values.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether eye worst weekly rout since FTX collapse as cryptos shed $390 billion
items.desc: A week that began with Strategy's bitcoin sale ended with one of the largest crypto market drawdowns in years.

items.title: Hyperion DeFi to unwind $29 million in HYPE deals with Felix, Native Markets as USDH sunsets
items.desc: Roughly 800,000 HYPE will return to the company's treasury for redeployment to "more profitable" strategies, per a Friday filing.

items.title: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.desc: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.

items.title: Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash: Can BTC rebound to $70K next?
items.desc: Bitcoin’s latest oversold RSI mirrors 2020 and February 2026 setups that preceded 50% and 30% rebounds, putting $70K back in focus.

items.title: America’s largest banks are building a new digital currency network to stop a massive deposit drain
items.desc: America’s biggest banks are launching tokenized deposits to compete with stablecoins, opening a new front in the race to become the dominant form of cash on blockchain networks.

items.title: A crypto pioneer who turned a $20 million family stake into a billion-dollar fund doubles down on bitcoin
items.desc: Sourcing initial capital from his mother to build a $1 billion crypto empire, DFG CEO James Wo says market metrics do not support Tom Lee’s $250,000 ether prediction.

items.title: Meet the Guys Still Clinging To the Terra Luna That Do Kwon Abandoned
items.desc: Terra Luna Classic was left behind after an epic collapse almost four years ago. But there’s a community of believers who haven’t given up.

items.title: Satoshi-era bitcoin at center of $285 billion lawsuit moves after 14 years
items.desc: The 1LwWt address received a legal notice from Salomon Brothers via Bitcoin's OP_RETURN field in July 2025 demanding the owner prove ownership by November 5, 2025.

items.title: Michael Saylor’s rallying cry: Bitcoin needs four forces to win
items.desc: The Strategy executive chairman argued that four distinct camps each play a vital role in bitcoin’s long-term success.

items.title: Cardano social activity surges as ADA falls under 20 cents to four-year lows
items.desc: Santiment data show active addresses at a four-month high and social dominance near a 2026 peak after Charles Hoskinson warned of a "wave of failures" in the ecosystem.
